title: An Ode, Or Psalm To God.
content:
Dear God,
    If Thy smart rod
    Here did not make me sorry,
    I should not be
    With Thine or Thee
    In Thy eternal glory.

            But since
    Thou didst convince
    My sins by gently striking;
    Add still to those
    First stripes new blows,
    According to Thy liking.

            Fear me,
    Or scourging tear me;
    That thus from vices driven,
    I may from hell
    Fly up to dwell
    With Thee and Thine in heaven.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "An Ode, Or Psalm To God."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
